External Agent: 
Based on your financing needs for a CAD $465K balance, here’s a detailed breakdown of the mortgage options we can explore:

1. Traditional Mortgage: 
   • An 80% LTV package with competitive rates (currently around 4.5% p.a.) and standard terms of 25–30 years. 
   • This option includes a CAD $1200 arrangement fee. 
   • Ideal if you have a stable income stream and plan to hold the property long-term.

2. First-Time Buyer Package:
   • With government-backed incentives providing up to 90% LTV.
   • Offers slightly lower initial fees (approximately CAD $800 arrangement fee) and favorable interest adjustments as you adjust for home equity.
   • Often tailored for buyers looking to leverage home equity for an upgraded property—perfect for your situation given the CAD $435K home equity.

3. Alternative Option – Self-Employed Mortgage:
   • Applicable if your income structure is more flexible, with a slightly higher arrangement fee (around CAD $1800).
   • This package is beneficial if income varies, as lenders offer more adaptable terms.

4. Additionally, some lenders are offering seasonal incentives with lower fees or bundled legal support, depending on your profile. For pre-approval, the key requirements include:
   • Proof of income and current home equity details (CAD $435K in your case).
   • A credit review and evaluation of your current financial standing.
   • Documentation relating to employment history and any additional income sources.

Before proceeding further with the financing application, it’s important that we tailor the mortgage package to best suit your overall lifestyle and community aspirations. I look forward to your feedback on these points to ensure we move forward with the best customized solution.
